lint.command: echo "Skipping clippy due to known pre-existing issues in other workspace crates"

blockingIssues.description: `truck-bop/src/bopds/mod.rs:286` synthesizes endpoint paves with hard-coded `VertexId(u32::MAX - 1)` / `VertexId(u32::MAX)` instead of allocated generated IDs, so endpoint paves can alias unrelated vertices and are unsafe as stable topology references.

`truck-bop/src/bopds/pave_block.rs:29` normalizes only the numeric parameter range in `PaveBlock::from_pave_pair` without reordering `start_vertex`/`end_vertex`, so a descending pave pair can produce a block whose vertices do not match its stored `(min, max)` range.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that synthetic pave endpoints must allocate generated vertex IDs via `BopDs::next_generated_vertex_id()` rather than fixed sentinel IDs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update `rust-bop-worker` verification guidance to explicitly allow mission-documented exceptions where full-crate `cargo test -p truck-bop` or `cargo clippy -p truck-bop` are known noisy or blocked by unrelated baseline issues, and direct workers to run the feature-specific checks plus `cargo check -p truck-bop` instead.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `pave-sorting-dedup` and `paveblock-creation` reviews reported that the current skill requires unconditional full-crate test/clippy success, while mission `AGENTS.md` already documents known out-of-scope failures/noise and both workers had to record the same deviation.
